Online Data Table VI: Quintile Transition Matrices by Commuting Zone

Notes: This table presents quintile transition matrices of child and parent income by CZ using the 1980-85 birth cohorts and the baseline family income definitions. Columns 3-27 report the probability that the child has family income in a given quintile of the national child income distribution conditional on having parents with family income in a given quintile of national parent income distribution. Columns 28-32 report the fraction of parents in the CZ that fall into the five national quintiles of the parent income distribution (the marginal distribution of parent income). Columns 33-37 report the fraction of children in the CZ that fall into the five national quintiles of the child income distribution (the marginal distribution of child income). In CZs where the number of children in any quintile-quintile bin is less than 10, we add random uniform noise over {-1, 0, 1} to all bin counts before we compute probabilities; CZs with at least 10 children in every quintile-quintile bin are not affected.
